title: (GATED) Plain-language "how to read ES survival numbers" explainer
context: This is a GATED, high-risk-tier task: a family-facing plain-language explainer on how to read ES survival numbers. Per CLAUDE.md refusal guardrails and the PLAN, it must NOT start unless an oncologist + patient-advocate reviewer pair is secured, must give NO individualized prognosis or medical advice, must source every claim to the dataset/literature, and requires recorded oncologist sign-off plus patient-advocate review before any publication. Strictly aggregate, de-identified, open-access data only; no patient-level data; not medical advice.
objective: Only if an oncologist + patient-advocate reviewer pair is secured: draft a plain-language, fully sourced explainer on how to read ES survival numbers, with prominent 'not medical advice', no individualized prognosis, gated behind recorded expert sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Not started unless an oncologist + patient-advocate reviewer pair is secured.
Every claim sourced to the dataset/literature; prominent 'not medical advice'; no individualized prognosis; written for families with care and plain language.
Oncologist sign-off AND patient-advocate review recorded before any publication.
